author: Milkhi
title: Kallu Kikkar Singh
gloss: Or an account of the famous wrestling match between Kallu of Amritsar and Kikkar Singh, the two champion wrestlers of the Punjab, held at Lahore in the amphitheatre of the Industrial and Agricultural Exhibition of the Punjab, North-West Frontier Province and Balúchistán during the year 1909-10.
notes: A verse account of a famous wrestling bout (Kallu vs Kikkar Singh) at the 1909-10 Lahore Exhibition — sporting spectacle as popular print; second Exhibition-themed qissa this quarter (cf. p16 s43).

author: Muhammad Azhar
title: Mukammal Qasas-ul-Muhsinin
gloss: Complete versified story of the Prophet Joseph and his beloved Zulaikha.
copyright: M. Ghulam Muhammad, Book-seller, Lahore; No. 20, dated the 14th January, 1910.
notes: A 684-page complete Yusuf-Zulaikha in Punjabi verse with dual binding prices — a monumental qissa (second Yusuf-Zulaikha this quarter after p11 s6).

author: Muhammad Din, M.
title: Hidayat Nama-i-Taun
gloss: A pamphlet which describes the causes of the spread of plague, and explains some preventive measures.
notes: Plague-prevention advice in Punjabi verse — public-health print during the ongoing plague epidemic; vernacular science/medicine.

printer_verbatim: Civil and Military Organ Press, Ludhiána.
author: Muhammad Shah
title: Pittan Nama
gloss: A small pamphlet directed against women who beat their breasts and weep aloud at funerals.
notes: Persian-script version of the same author's Pittan Nama (cf. p13 s22, Nagari-side) — anti-ritual-mourning reform verse; the same text across script markets.

author: Muqbil
title: Madh-i-Pir Zal
gloss: Verses in praise of Shaikh Abdul Qadir Jilani, the well-known Muhammadan saint of Baghdád.
notes: Praise of Abd al-Qadir Jilani (Qadiri Sufi founder) — Hindu bookseller (Anand) publishing Muslim saint-praise.

author: Nihal Singh
title: Halat-i-Hoshiárpur
gloss: Verses describing the work of the Singh Sabha, Hoshiárpur.
notes: Verses celebrating the local Singh Sabha's work — Sikh reform self-documentation. Reg 570 is anomalously high for a Jan-Mar 1910 entry; likely a misread (possibly 70 or 170) — flagged.

flags.issue: printed reg reads 570, far above the quarter's ~1-383 range; likely misread of a smaller number (e.g. 70/170) — transcribed as printed
